_meta.purpose: GQM-based quality measurement schema for V1 (Skill Quality) and V3 (Output Quality)
assumptions: Process metrics ≠ quality metrics (Anthropic eval guide)
Self-assessment degrades accuracy (ICLR 2024 Huang et al.)
Hypotheses must be defined before data collection (GQM Basili)
Adversarial test required for all metrics

q1_structural_contribution.text: Are skills producing structurally non-trivial improvements?

theorem_delta_per_run.description: Net change in Lean theorem count per evolve run
theorem_delta_per_run.adversarial_test: Can be gamed by adding trivial theorems — mitigated by R5 non-triviality gate

test_delta_per_run.description: Net change in acceptance test count per evolve run
test_delta_per_run.adversarial_test: Can be gamed by adding trivial tests — mitigated by R5 non-triviality gate

q2_verification_quality.text: Are skill proposals passing independent verification?

verifier_pass_rate.description: Ratio of verifier-passed proposals to total verified proposals
verifier_pass_rate.adversarial_test: Can be gamed by lowering verifier standards — mitigated by R4 E1 separation

rejected_count_per_run.description: Number of rejected proposals per evolve run
rejected_count_per_run.adversarial_test: Can be gamed by not proposing ambitious changes — cross-validate with q1 metrics

evolve_success_rate.description: Percentage of evolve runs completing successfully (EXISTING proxy, reclassified as process metric)
evolve_success_rate.reclassification_note: Was primary V1 proxy. Now reclassified as process metric under Q3, not quality metric.
evolve_success_rate.adversarial_test: 98% success with trivial improvements is possible — q1 metrics are the quality check

failure_type_distribution.description: Distribution of failure types in rejected proposals
failure_type_distribution.hypothesis: hypothesis_error is most frequent (indicates ambitious proposals, not systemic issues)

test_pass_rate.description: Percentage of acceptance tests passing (EXISTING proxy, retained)
test_pass_rate.adversarial_test: 100% with weak tests is meaningless — mitigated by R6 saturation detection

lean_build_health.description: Binary: Lean build succeeds with 0 sorry
lean_build_health.adversarial_test: Can be maintained by never adding new Lean code — cross-validate with v1.q1 theorem delta

axiom_count_stability.description: Lean axiom count should not decrease (axioms are foundational)
axiom_count_stability.adversarial_test: Axiom inflation is possible but costly (requires Lean type-checking)

compatibility_classification.source: git commit messages (P3 hook enforces classification)
compatibility_classification.adversarial_test: Mislabeling is possible — verifier should cross-check

q3_error_trend.text: Is the error rate decreasing over time?

hallucination_total.description: Total count of typed rejected proposals (observation_error + hypothesis_error + assumption_error + precondition_error)

loopback_count_per_run.description: Total loopback count (re-attempts due to errors) per run

governance_metrics._description: Metrics that are OBSERVED but NEVER used as optimization targets in the /evolve loop. Their purpose is post-hoc validation of improvement quality. If optimization metrics improve but governance metrics degrade, Goodhart pressure is likely active.
governance_metrics._enforcement: SKILL.md explicitly labels these as governance. Observer reports them; Hypothesizer must NOT propose improvements that target them directly.

test_pass_count.description: Absolute number of acceptance tests passing (not the rate)
test_pass_count.gaming_resistance: Adding trivial tests inflates count — mitigated by R5 non-triviality gate
test_pass_count.why_governance: Test count is a ceiling indicator. Optimizing it directly incentivizes trivial test additions.

lean_axiom_count.description: Total axiom count in Lean formalization
lean_axiom_count.gaming_resistance: Adding axioms requires Lean type-checking — high cost to game
lean_axiom_count.why_governance: Axioms are foundational claims. Increasing them without justification weakens the formal system.

lean_theorem_count.description: Total theorem count in Lean formalization
lean_theorem_count.gaming_resistance: Theorems must type-check — medium cost to game with trivial theorems
lean_theorem_count.why_governance: Theorem count is used in V1.q1 as a DELTA (optimization). The ABSOLUTE count is governance — it validates that deltas are accumulating correctly.

lean_sorry_count.description: Count of sorry (unproven) in Lean — must remain 0
lean_sorry_count.gaming_resistance: Cannot be gamed upward unintentionally; zero is the only valid value
lean_sorry_count.why_governance: This is a CONSTRAINT, not an optimization target. The agent should never 'try to reduce sorry' — it should never introduce sorry in the first place.

de_bruijn_factor.description: Ratio of formal lines to informal specification lines (≈4.0 is healthy)
de_bruijn_factor.gaming_resistance: Changing this requires significant Lean code changes
de_bruijn_factor.why_governance: Optimizing this directly would incentivize verbose proofs (increase) or deleting proofs (decrease).

v2_context_efficiency.description: Tool calls per session — hub variable with tradeoffs against V1,V3,V5,V6,V7
v2_context_efficiency.gaming_resistance: Low — agent could reduce tool calls by doing less work
v2_context_efficiency.why_governance: V2 is a hub variable. Optimizing it directly degrades V1 and V3 (tradeoff_context_is_hub in Observable.lean).

governance_metrics.cross_validation_rule: When v1.q1 metrics (theorem_delta, test_delta) show improvement but governance metrics (absolute counts) are flat or declining, investigate for Goodhart pressure.

proxy_correlation._description: Rolling Pearson correlation between optimization proxies and structural outcomes. Weakening correlation signals possible Goodhart pressure. Run: bash .claude/skills/evolve/scripts/proxy-correlation.sh

baseline_2026_03_27.interpretation: Weak overall correlations expected at n=55. 'weakening' trends in theorem-related pairs likely reflect diminishing low-hanging fruit rather than Goodhart pressure, as governance metrics (R2) show healthy growth. Monitor for divergence as data accumulates.

non_triviality_gate._description: Rule-based assessment of whether an evolve run produced structurally non-trivial improvements. Score 0-4 based on conditions met. Prevents Goodhart gaming where metrics improve but improvements are trivial.

c1_theorem_growth.rationale: Adding a Lean theorem requires type-checking — structurally non-trivial

c2_test_growth.rationale: Adding acceptance tests extends the quality surface

c3_axiom_change.rationale: Axiom changes are foundational — always non-trivial

c4_multi_verification.rationale: Multiple independently verified improvements indicate breadth

baseline_2026_03_27.note: 54% of runs are trivial by this definition — expected for a mature system where low-hanging fruit is depleted

saturation_detection._description: Detects when metrics lose discriminative power due to ceiling effects. When test suite stops growing while pass rate stays at 100%, the metric stops providing useful quality signal.

test_plateau_warning.action: Observer should flag test suite growth stall as improvement candidate

test_saturation_alert.action: Mandatory: new test categories must be proposed in next evolve run

baseline_2026_03_27.note: Longest historical plateau was 20 runs at 165 tests. Current growth rate (last 10 runs avg 3.3 tests/run) suggests saturation is not imminent.

priority_bias._description: V1-V7 間の優先順位は静的に導出不可能 (G1b-1 #91 証明済み)。priority(V_i, V_j) = g(f(t), bias(t)) モデルに従い、bias(t) は T6 権限を持つ主体が設定する。このセクションは bias(t) のスナップショットであり、必ず陳腐化する。
priority_bias._model: priority(V_i, V_j) = g(f(t), bias(t)) where f(t) = system state function, bias(t) = priority bias function

f_t_observations.system_phase: stable operation — defect absence achieved, non-triviality is next bottleneck

priority_order.reason: proxy graduation is the project goal (#77). defect absence achieved but proxy still provisional
GQM redefined (R1) but quality definition pending confirmation
100% = Goodhart example. gate_blocked logging not implemented. signal non-functional
hub variable — monitor but do not optimize directly (tradeoff with V1/V3)

review_policy.description: bias(t) is a function of time and WILL become stale. Mandatory review triggers:

triggers.condition: V1 or V3 proxy graduates to formal
20 runs elapsed since last review (next: Run 83)

review_policy.next_mandatory_review: Run 83 or V1/V3 formal graduation, whichever comes first

v1_skill_invocation_rate.reason: Measures 'are skills used?' not 'are skills producing quality outputs?' — GQM goal-question mismatch

v3_fix_ratio_percent.reason: Regex pattern broken for this project's commit style ([evolve] Fix...). fix_ratio stuck at 0%
v3_fix_ratio_percent.action: Remove from observe.sh or fix regex
